testPlan.title: TP: Verify share.create NFS export creation across MCP → daemon → kernel stack
testPlan.scope: Full share.create flow — Zod schema validation, plan/apply middleware, preflight checks (path existence, duplicate detection), NFS option building (security, async, RDMA), Unix socket delegation to nfs-helper daemon, /etc/exports file manipulation with fcntl locking, exportfs -r kernel reload. Excludes: share.delete, share.update_policy, share.set_quota, Ansible exports role, shell menu scripts.

risks.description: Silent data exposure — incorrect NFS options (missing sync, wrong sec=) could expose data without encryption or allow async writes that lose data on crash
/etc/exports corruption — concurrent writes or parse failures could make all NFS exports unavailable
Daemon unavailability — if nfs-helper socket is down, share.create fails with opaque timeout instead of actionable error
Idempotency violation — overwriting an existing export without warning could silently change access policy for active clients
exportfs -r failure — export written to file but not loaded into kernel means share appears created but is not mountable
Path traversal — exporting sensitive system paths (e.g., /etc, /root) without guardrails

testPlan.environment: Ubuntu 22.04/24.04 LTS, nfs-kernel-server installed, xinas-nfs-helper.service running, at least one XFS filesystem mounted at /mnt/data, RDMA-capable NIC for TC-006
testPlan.entryCriteria: xiNAS-MCP server running with valid config, nfs-helper daemon listening on Unix socket, target export directory exists on filesystem
testPlan.exitCriteria: All P0 and P1 test cases pass. No exports file corruption observed. Every created export is mountable from a client.

testCases.title: Verify share.create happy path creates mountable NFS export
testCases.preconditions: /mnt/data/share1 exists, nfs-helper daemon running, no existing export for this path
testCases.inputData: { path: "/mnt/data/share1", clients: [{ host: "*", options: ["rw","no_root_squash"] }], security: "sys", mode: "apply" }

steps.action: Call share.create with input data in mode: apply
From NFS client, run mount -t nfs server:/mnt/data/share1 /mnt/test

testCases.expectedResult: Export is written to /etc/exports, loaded into kernel via exportfs -r, and mountable by NFS clients

testCases.title: Verify plan mode returns preflight result without side effects
testCases.preconditions: /mnt/data/share_plan exists, no export for this path

steps.expected: Returns PlanResult with mode: plan, preflight_passed: true
Has action: create, resource_type: nfs_export, after.path matches input

testCases.expectedResult: PlanResult returned with accurate description; no changes to filesystem or kernel export table

testCases.title: Verify share.create blocks when path does not exist

steps.action: Call share.create with mode: plan for nonexistent path
steps.expected: Returns PlanResult with preflight_passed: false, blocking_resources includes 'Path does not exist'

testCases.expectedResult: Apply mode throws preflight error; no export is created for nonexistent path
testCases.observability: Error message contains 'Path does not exist', /etc/exports unchanged

testCases.title: Verify duplicate export overwrites with warning

steps.action: Call share.create with mode: plan for existing export
steps.expected: PlanResult warnings include 'already exists — will be overwritten'

steps.action: Count entries for /mnt/data/existing in exports file

testCases.expectedResult: Existing export replaced atomically; warning surfaced in plan; only one entry in exports file

testCases.title: Verify buildNfsOptions applies security mode correctly
testCases.inputData: { path: "/mnt/data/secure", clients: [{ host: "*", options: ["rw"] }], security: "krb5i", mode: "apply" }

steps.action: Create another export with security: sys (default)
steps.expected: Options do NOT include sec= prefix

testCases.expectedResult: Non-default security modes append sec=<mode>; sys mode omits sec= parameter

testCases.title: Verify RDMA option is appended when rdma=true
testCases.inputData: { path: "/mnt/data/rdma_share", clients: [{ host: "*", options: ["rw"] }], rdma: true, mode: "apply" }

steps.action: Create export with rdma: false (default) on different path

testCases.expectedResult: RDMA option present in exports only when explicitly enabled

testCases.title: Verify async_commit=true sets async instead of sync
testCases.inputData: { path: "/mnt/data/async_share", clients: [{ host: "*", options: ["rw"] }], async_commit: true, mode: "apply" }

steps.expected: Options include async, do NOT include sync
Options include sync, do NOT include async

testCases.expectedResult: Mutually exclusive async/sync option applied based on async_commit boolean

testCases.title: Verify Zod rejects empty clients array

steps.action: Call share.create with empty clients array
steps.expected: Zod validation error thrown before handler executes
Indicates array must have at least 1 element

testCases.expectedResult: Zod .min(1) constraint rejects empty clients; no side effects

testCases.title: Verify share.create fails gracefully when daemon is down

steps.expected: Fails with McpToolError containing 'Cannot connect to nfs-helper' and 'Is xinas-nfs-helper.service running?'

testCases.expectedResult: Actionable error message with service hint; fails within timeout; no partial state

testCases.title: Verify /etc/exports is written atomically with fcntl lock
testCases.preconditions: nfs-helper daemon running, 2 concurrent MCP clients

steps.action: Send two share.create requests concurrently for different paths
Verify no truncated or malformed lines

testCases.expectedResult: Concurrent writes serialize correctly via fcntl locking; no data loss or corruption

testCases.title: Verify exportfs -r failure does not leave stale export file entry

steps.expected: Entry IS written (file written before reload — design gap)

steps.action: Restore binary and run exportfs -r manually

testCases.expectedResult: Error indicates missing nfs-kernel-server. Note: file is written before kernel reload, leaving inconsistent state on failure — known design gap.
testCases.observability: daemon stderr logs, /etc/exports vs exportfs -v comparison

testCases.title: Verify multi-client export renders correctly in /etc/exports
testCases.inputData: { path: "/mnt/data/multi", clients: [{ host: "10.0.0.0/24", options: ["rw"] }, { host: "192.168.1.0/24", options: ["ro"] }], mode: "apply" }

steps.action: Call share.create with two clients having different options

steps.expected: Single line with both clients space-separated, each with own options
Shows both client networks with respective permissions

testCases.expectedResult: Multiple clients rendered correctly. Note: buildNfsOptions uses clients[0].options for all clients — second client's ro option may be overridden. Verify actual behavior.

testCases.title: Verify idempotency — creating same export twice yields single entry
testCases.inputData: Same params twice: { path: "/mnt/data/idem", clients: [{ host: "*", options: ["rw"] }], mode: "apply" }

steps.action: Call share.create second time with identical params

testCases.expectedResult: Repeated creation is idempotent — replaces existing entry rather than duplicating

testCases.title: Verify share.create with all four Kerberos security modes
testCases.inputData: Four calls with security: sys, krb5, krb5i, krb5p respectively, mode: plan

steps.action: Call share.create with security: sys in plan mode
steps.expected: PlanResult options do NOT include sec= prefix

testCases.expectedResult: All four security modes accepted by Zod; only non-sys values produce sec= option in export options

testCases.title: Verify path with spaces is handled correctly in exports file
testCases.inputData: { path: "/mnt/data/my share", clients: [{ host: "*", options: ["rw"] }], mode: "apply" }

steps.action: Call share.create with path containing spaces

steps.expected: Path is properly formatted (quoted or escaped)

testCases.expectedResult: Paths with spaces serialized correctly. Note: current _serialize_exports does NOT quote paths — this test likely reveals a bug.
